stem: A pond was 30 centimeters deep when its depth was first measured. The equation 30 + 3n = 51 can be used to find how many years n it took the pond to reach a depth of 51 centimeters. Which of the following is the best interpretation of the number 3 in this context?

answerOptions.content: The number of years it took the pond to gain 51 cm of depth
The depth, in centimeters, of the pond when first measured
The average number of centimeters the pond gained in depth per year

rationale: The equation has the form (initial depth) + (rate per year) · n = (final depth). The coefficient 3 multiplies n (years), so 3 represents the average yearly increase in depth, in centimeters. Choice D is correct.
